principles: A good day is defined by a few high-leverage moves, not a full checklist.
Energy and constraints must shape the plan.
Buffers are necessary; days rarely go perfectly.

playbooks.name: Daily plan
steps: Place them in the day around fixed constraints.

constraints: Avoid stuffing the day with more than can realistically fit.
Do not ignore sleep, food, and recovery.

examples.situation: User wants to do 12 deep work tasks in one day.
examples.response: Consolidate into 2–3 deep work blocks and push the rest to later days.
